title: Inconsistent case sensitivity in TX/RX detection
description:
## Location
scripts/analyze-hetero-fleet.py:60

## Description
The detection logic uses mixed case handling: '[TX]' is matched case-sensitively while 'TX:' uses line.upper() for case-insensitive matching. This inconsistency means '[tx]' is missed while 'tx:' is caught. Different implementations may use different casing conventions.

## Failure Scenario
A Python implementation logs '[tx] hash=ABC123' (lowercase brackets). The check for '[TX]' fails (case-sensitive), and '[tx]' doesn't match 'TX:' even with .upper(). The packet is not counted, causing under-reporting of Python TX statistics.

## Category
parsing
close_reason: Case-insensitive TX/RX detection fixed via line.upper() + re.IGNORECASE across python/rust/zephyr parsers + telemetry. Streaming open() implemented to address memory P1 from reviews. P4 fixed: typing imports, duplicate splitlines loops combined, dead code removed, regex unified. 3 complete codereview passes completed with all findings filed and resolved (no P0-P3 left in script). Parsing tested with mixed-case logs successfully. bd ready --limit 5 run.
